verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 (inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor type 1) is an ER-resident multi-pass TM Ca2+ release channel. Its TM domains span the ER membrane with the large IP3-binding cytoplasmic domain facing the cytosol and the channel pore releasing Ca2+ from ER lumen to cytosol. No cell-state-induced PM ecto-display documented; no narrow germline/developmental surface lineage; not a lysosomal TM protein undergoing exocytosis; no documented PM minority pool or ER-PM-junction outer-leaflet exposure (ER-PM junctions keep it on the ER face); no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering at the cell surface. Canonical ER membrane channel.
